title: DAY 1: Foundations – What Is Copywriting & Why It Works
objective: By the end of Day 1, you will understand what copywriting really is, the psychology behind it, and write your first piece of persuasive copy.

detailedContent.title: Copywriting Is Salesmanship in Print
detailedContent.content:
Copywriting is not “creative writing.” It's **salesmanship in print** – using words to get people to take action.

**The Core Purpose of Copy:**
- Grabs attention
- Holds interest
- Builds desire
- Prompts action

**Psychology of Persuasion:**
- **Features vs. Benefits:** People don't buy mattresses; they buy a good night's sleep.
- **Pain and Pleasure:** Move users away from a problem and toward a solution.
- **Emotional Drivers:** Decisions are made emotionally and justified logically.

detailedContent.title: The AIDA Framework
detailedContent.content:
AIDA mirrors how people process information:
1. **Attention:** Hook them with the first sentence.
2. **Interest:** Share engaging details or relevance.
3. **Desire:** Show the benefits and life after the solve.
4. **Action:** A clear instruction on what to do next.

video.title: The Ultimate Beginner's Guide to Copywriting
video.description: Flux Academy breaks down the fundamentals of salesmanship in print.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Feature to Benefit Transformation (10 minutes)
steps: Convert technical features (e.g., '5-minute setup') into human benefits (e.g., 'Spend more time working, less time configuring')

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Write Your First AIDA Copy (15 minutes)
steps: Pick a simple object (like a mug or pen) and write an AIDA-structured paragraph for it
Apply the 'So What?' test to ensure you reached the deepest benefit

reflectionPrompt: How is copywriting different from what I expected? Which part of AIDA felt hardest?

title: DAY 2: Headlines That Stop the Scroll
objective: By the end of Day 2, you will be able to write magnetic headlines using proven formulas and techniques.

detailedContent.title: The Headline Is 80% of the Work
detailedContent.content:
Five times as many people read the headline as the body copy. If the headline fails, the rest is unread.

**Proven Formulas:**
- **How-To:** 'How to Write Headlines That Sell'
- **Numbers:** '7 Ways to Double Your Productivity'
- **Curiosity Gap:** 'The One Word That Changes Everything'
- **Problem-Solve:** 'The Sleep‑Better‑Tonight Solution'

**Best Practices:** Be specific, use numbers, and address the reader directly.

video.title: 19 Headline Formulas That Work
video.description: Ray Edwards shares time-tested templates to grab immediate attention.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Headline Deconstruction (10 minutes)
steps: Find 10 headlines in your inbox or YouTube and identify which formulas they use

exercises.title: Exercise 2: The Headline Challenge (20 minutes)
steps: Pick one product and write 20 different headlines for it using various formulas
Select your top 3 and refine the winner for maximum impact

reflectionPrompt: What was hardest about writing 20 headlines for a single product?

title: DAY 3: The Art of Storytelling in Copy
objective: By the end of Day 3, you will be able to weave short, powerful stories into your copy to build connection.

detailedContent.title: Stories Are How Humans Think
detailedContent.content:
Stories stick because they trigger emotion and build trust. 

**The PAS Framework:**
- **Problem:** State the user's struggle.
- **Agitate:** Make the pain feel real (frustration, cost of inaction).
- **Solve:** Present your solution as the path to relief.

**Story Arc in Copy:** The customer is the Hero, the struggle is the Villain, and your product is the Guide.

video.title: The Magical Science of Storytelling
video.description: David JP Phillips explains why our brains are hardwired for narrative.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Write a PAS Sequence (15 minutes)
steps: Choose a service and write a 3-paragraph Problem-Agitate-Solve sequence

exercises.title: Exercise 2: The Mini-Story (10 minutes)
steps: Write a 4-sentence personal story that leads directly into a product benefit

reflectionPrompt: How does storytelling feel different from regular descriptive copy?

title: DAY 4: Writing for Different Platforms & Formats
objective: By the end of Day 4, you will understand how to adapt your copy for social, email, and web.

detailedContent.title: One Message, Many Formats
detailedContent.content:
**Social Media:** Conversational, visual-first, short.
**Email:** Personalized, subject line is headline, one main CTA.
**Website/Landing Pages:** Above-the-fold punch, scannable with bullets, heavy on social proof.
**Ads:** Extremely concise, immediate hook, clear offer.

video.title: How to Write for Different Platforms
video.description: Alex Cattoni breaks down the differences between an Instagram post and a Sales Email.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Format Adaptation (20 minutes)
steps: Write 4 variations of the same offer: a 150-character social post, a 3-paragraph email, a Landing Page hero section, and a 2-line ad

reflectionPrompt: Which platform felt most natural to write for? What was the hardest part of condensing the message?

title: DAY 5: The Mechanics – Editing & Polishing
objective: By the end of Day 5, you will be able to edit your copy ruthlessly to eliminate fluff.

detailedContent.title: Writing Is Rewriting
detailedContent.content:
The first draft is just you talking to yourself. The edit is where you sell.

**The Editing Checklist:**
- **Clarity:** Is it simple enough for a 12-year-old?
- **Conciseness:** Can it be said in 20% fewer words?
- **Flow:** Does it have a rhythm? Try the 'Read Aloud' test.
- **Tone:** Is it consistent and brand-appropriate?
- **Grammar:** Use tools like Grammarly but trust your ear.

video.title: How to Edit Your Copy Like a Pro
video.description: Practical tips for sharpening your sentences and killing your darlings.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Ruthless Reduction (15 minutes)
steps: Take your Day 4 email draft and cut 30% of the word count without losing the meaning

exercises.title: Exercise 2: The Read-Aloud Test (5 minutes)
steps: Record yourself reading your edited copy. Identify any spots where you stumble—those need more editing.

reflectionPrompt: What is the most common filler word I tend to use (just, very, really)?

title: DAY 6: Crafting Compelling Calls to Action (CTAs)
objective: By the end of Day 6, you will be able to write powerful CTAs that drive measurable results.

detailedContent.title: Tell Them Exactly What to Do Next
detailedContent.content:
In the absence of clear instructions, people do nothing.

**Strong CTA Anatomy:**
1. **Action Verb:** Get, Start, Claim, Download.
2. **Immediate Benefit:** 'Get Your Free Guide' vs 'Download'.
3. **Low Risk:** 'No credit card required'.
4. **Urgency:** 'Offer expires tonight'.

**The One-CTA Rule:** Every piece of copy should have ONE primary goal. Don't confuse the reader with too many choices.

video.title: CTA Formulas That Work
video.description: Ray Edwards shares templates for writing buttons and links that people actually click.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: CTA Variation Challenge (10 minutes)
steps: Write 10 different CTA variations for a single free ebook offer

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Weak to Strong Transformation (10 minutes)
steps: Improve 5 weak CTAs like 'Submit', 'Click Here', and 'Buy Now' using benefits and action verbs

reflectionPrompt: Why is 'Sign Up' a weaker call to action than 'Start My Free Trial'?

title: DAY 7: Final Project – Complete Sales piece
objective: By the end of Day 7, you will have written a complete, polished sales asset for your portfolio.

detailedContent.title: The Final Deadline
detailedContent.content:
**Final Project Options:**
- **A: Short Sales Page:** Headline, body, testimonial, CTA.
- **B: 3-Email Sequence:** Welcome, Story, Offer.
- **C: Social Mini-Campaign:** 5 cohesive daily posts.

**Final Checklist:**
1. Magnetic headline.
2. Benefit-driven (Features -> Benefits).
3. Emotional storytelling element.
4. Clear, strong CTA.
5. Ruthlessly edited for clarity.

video.title: The Perfect Sales Page Formula
video.description: A start-to-finish walkthrough of writing a high-converting long-form page.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Master Copy (90+ minutes)
steps: Plan -> Draft -> Edit your final sales project
Assemble it into a clean document ready for a client or portfolio

reflectionPrompt: Looking back at Day 1, what is the biggest shift in how I think about writing?
